title: Syrian Eggplant Biryani
description: A richly flavored lacto-vegetarian Syrian dish featuring coconut flakes and miso-marinated eggplant, perfect for a crowd, with a grassy and gooey texture that is sure to please. This dish is a classic example of Syrian cuisine's ability to balance flavors and textures.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together miso paste, vegetable oil, and yogurt to make the marinade.
Add the eggplant cubes to the marinade and toss to coat.
Let the eggplant marinate for at least 30 minutes.
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the marinated eggplant to the skillet and cook until it is tender.
In a large pot, bring 4 cups of water to a boil.
Add the basmati rice to the pot and cook until it is tender.
Fluff the cooked rice with a fork and stir in the coconut flakes.
In a large serving dish, combine the cooked eggplant and rice.
